rag_rules: Repair AGREE expressions by aligning types and fixing unresolved references found in validator diagnostics.
Ensure AGREE contracts use valid component type scopes and avoid implementation-level bindings in type annexes.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the current AADL scope and properly declared.
generic_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations in AGREE contracts.
Place component type assumptions and guarantees only in valid component type scopes.
Preserve type consistency in AGREE expressions by matching declared types with actual values.
Avoid referencing AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `const` for constant declarations in AGREE.
Guard previous-value comparisons with an initialized boolean flag when using `pre()` expressions.
Ensure all AGREE expressions are well-typed and do not contain `<error>` types.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference package-qualified identifiers without matching `with` clauses in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean or opaque data types into numeric values for comparisons.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in component implementations
Component implementation-level bindings in type contracts
